apps/workbench/styles.css.reason: Legacy workbench CSS: static shell layout, responsive rules, and state styling remain coupled until the Next.js workbench migration fully replaces the static shell.

apps/workbench/workbench.js.reason: Legacy static workbench shell: projection rendering, event controls, and mobile/desktop compatibility are kept together while Next.js routes are still being accepted.

legacy/root-workflow-guard/agent-workflo.reason: Archived root workflow guard migration input. It is not a default runtime module and should not grow except for migration quarantine fixes.

src/workflow/agent-lifecycle-pool.js.reason: Agent lifecycle pool domain module: records lifecycle facts, cleanup policy, and projection inputs across one state contract.

src/workflow/agent-invocation.js.reason: Agent invocation entrypoint: keeps provider profile resolution, CLI command construction, idle-aware process execution (with guaranteed lock release and SIGKILL escalation), and output normalization together for all model-backed flows.

src/workflow/autonomous-continuation.js.reason: Autonomous continuation planner: consolidates next-action selection, work package generation, and governance recovery decisions.

src/workflow/autonomous-orchestrator.js.reason: Autonomous orchestrator composition: wires scheduling, provider invocation, and durable run outputs through one bounded orchestration entry.

src/workflow/autonomous-scheduler-loop.j.reason: Scheduler loop contract implementation: loop execution, recovery, projection reuse, artifact validation, and execution-root propagation are still tightly coupled.

src/workflow/context-work-package-execut.reason: Context work package execution adapter: maps execution profiles, provider transport, and structured worker outputs.

src/workflow/context-work-package-provid.reason: Context work package provider executor: maps provider CLI command construction, depth-bounded streamed result parsing, provenance, and package-level pass/fail evidence.

src/workflow/context-work-package-runner.reason: Context work package runner: execution-scope and workspace mutation guard logic has been extracted to src/workflow/context-work-package-execution-scope.js; execution governance, worker dispatch, execution-root provenance, and result normalization still share one flow.

src/workflow/context-work-package-execut.reason: Extracted context work package execution-scope helper kept below the large-file threshold while preserving code-output isolation, primary-worktree blocking, no-code workspace mutation guard, and git porcelain snapshot behavior.

src/workflow/development-flow-real.js.reason: Development flow real acceptance harness: fixture setup, dual CLI command construction, provider C2C dispatch governance, model-output contract parsing, and phase evidence aggregation are kept together for the reusable closeout gate.

src/workflow/frontend-acceptance.js.reason: Frontend acceptance validator: browser evidence schema, layout/content checks, console errors, and live-route constraints are one acceptance surface.
src/workflow/frontend-acceptance.js.growth_justification: LFG-P6.2 Next.js acceptance hardening adds a fail-closed guard that rejects nextjs_app_router artifacts unless legacy_static_shell_used is explicitly false.

src/workflow/headless-cli-orchestrator.j.reason: Headless CLI orchestrator: worker planning and child worker acceptance/result packaging have been extracted to bounded modules; runner dispatch, process hardening, snapshot/continuation packaging, and projected-action service execution still need staged extraction.

src/workflow/headless-child-acceptance.j.reason: Extracted Headless CLI child worker acceptance helper kept below the large-file threshold while preserving host-boundary, owned-file, no-diff, command-evidence, durable-state, process-hardening, continuation, and self-evaluation checks.

src/workflow/headless-worker-planning.js.reason: Extracted Headless CLI worker planning helper kept below the large-file threshold while preserving work-package selection and child lifecycle spawn fact shape.

src/workflow/live-route-acceptance.js.reason: Live route acceptance: validates public route evidence, auth redirect state, freshness, and durable status integration.

src/workflow/requirement-intake.js.reason: Requirement intake pipeline: extracts structured requirements, recovery state, and workbench-ready artifacts from one evolving contract.

src/workflow/workbench-projection.js.reason: Workbench projection composition: core aggregation remains while next-action readout and project-management readout policy have been extracted to bounded modules; remaining projection-level orchestration is kept as the projection composer.

test/autonomous-continuation.test.js.reason: Autonomous continuation regression suite shares fixtures for next-action, recovery, reviewer, and work package dispatch behavior.

test/autonomous-scheduler-loop.test.js.reason: Scheduler loop tests share replay, recovery, and continuation fixtures for the same loop contract.

test/context-work-package-provider-execu.reason: Provider executor tests share mock provider and structured output fixtures across success, timeout, depth-bounded parsing, and error paths.

test/context-work-package-runner.test.js.reason: Context work package runner tests share owned-file, dependency, and execution governance fixtures.

test/frontend-acceptance.test.js.reason: Frontend acceptance tests cover content, layout, console, mounted route, favicon, and live-route false-pass regressions over shared artifacts.
test/frontend-acceptance.test.js.growth_justification: LFG-P6.2 Next.js acceptance hardening adds regression coverage for legacy static shell false-pass rejection.

test/headless-cli-orchestrator.test.js.reason: Headless CLI orchestrator tests share worker-result, owned-file, no-diff, and continuation acceptance fixtures.

test/workbench-shell.test.js.reason: Workbench shell tests now cover the Next.js/Ant Design shell contract, mounted runtime replacement gates, hook/API regressions, operation readouts, responsive overflow guards, and scheduler writeback invariants without reading legacy desktop/mobile shell assets.

test/workbench-projection-source.test.js.reason: Projection source tests share fixture coverage for dynamic and static workbench projection inputs.

test/workbench-projection.test.js.reason: Workbench projection tests cover the full projection schema, provider failure attribution, project-management fixtures, mobile projection, and many regression fixtures; one-screen helper counter coverage was extracted to test/workbench-projection-one-screen.test.js, and further staged split should follow schema subdomains.

test/workbench-server.test.js.reason: Workbench server tests cover mounted static assets, APIs, state store behavior, isolated execution-root propagation, public-route evidence, and event/snapshot flows over one server fixture. Agent key route coverage was extracted to test/workbench-server-agent-key-routes.test.js as the first test shard; remaining route/state/static domains still require staged splitting.

tools/workbench-server.mjs.reason: Workbench server entrypoint wires API handlers, live state stores, historical task preservation, provider dispatch execution-root propagation, and mounted public-route behavior while the server is still a single operational boundary. Agent key and agent health routes were extracted to tools/workbench-agent-key-routes.mjs; legacy static/mounted compatibility routing was extracted to tools/workbench-static-routes.mjs; remaining route groups still require staged extraction.

src/workflow/workbench-next-action-reado.reason: Extracted Workbench next-action readout domain helper kept below the large-file threshold while preserving projection output shape.

src/workflow/workbench-project-managemen.reason: Extracted Workbench project-management readout aggregation kept below the large-file threshold while preserving project counters, managed projects, plan-review, and task-flow output shape.

src/workflow/workbench-project-task-item.reason: Extracted Workbench project-management task item, plan-review, and requirement work-package readout helpers kept below the large-file threshold.
